content:
For the elevation and temperature data given in the table, the existing lapse rate in the environment is _____ ℃/100 m (round off to two decimal places).

$$ \begin{array}{|c|c|} \hline \text { Elevation from ground level (m) } & \text { Temperature }\left({ }^{\circ} \mathbf{C}\right) \\ \hline 5 & 14.2 \\ \hline 325 & 16.9 \\ \hline \end{array} $$
explanation:
To calculate the lapse rate, we need to determine the change in temperature per unit change in elevation. Let's start by identifying the values and then performing the necessary calculations:

We have the following data:

Elevation from ground level (m) Temperature (°C)
5 14.2
325 16.9

To find the lapse rate, use the formula:

$$ \text{Lapse Rate} = \frac{\Delta T}{\Delta h} $$

Where:

  • $$ \Delta T $$ is the change in temperature
  • $$ \Delta h $$ is the change in elevation

Calculating the changes:

$$ \Delta T = 16.9\,^{\circ}\text{C} - 14.2\,^{\circ}\text{C} = 2.7\,^{\circ}\text{C} $$

$$ \Delta h = 325\,\text{m} - 5\,\text{m} = 320\,\text{m} $$

Now, substituting these values into the lapse rate formula:

$$ \text{Lapse Rate} = \frac{2.7\,^{\circ}\text{C}}{320\,\text{m}} = 0.0084375\,^{\circ}\text{C/m} $$

We need to express the lapse rate in units of ℃/100 m. Therefore, multiply by 100:

$$ \text{Lapse Rate} = 0.0084375\,^{\circ}\text{C/m} \times 100 = 0.84375\,^{\circ}\text{C/100 m} $$

Rounding off to two decimal places:

$$ \text{Lapse Rate} \approx 0.84\,^{\circ}\text{C/100 m} $$

Thus, the existing lapse rate in the environment is approximately 0.84 °C/100 m.

content:
Match the following attributes of a city with the appropriate scale of measurements.

Attribute Scale of measurement
(P) Average temperature ($$^\circ $$C) of a city (I) Interval
(Q) Name of city (II) Oridnal
(R) Population density of a city (III) Nominal
(S) Ranking of a city based on ease of business (IV) Ratio

Which one of the following combinations is correct?
explanation:
Option C is correct.

Here are the correct pairings :

(P) Average temperature $\left( ^\circ C \right)$ of a city - (I) Interval : Temperature in degrees Celsius represents interval data because it does not have a true zero point and the distances between numbers are meaningful.

(Q) Name of city - (III) Nominal : Names of cities represent nominal data as they are categories without any quantitative value or order.

(R) Population density of a city - (IV) Ratio : Population density is a ratio scale because it has a true zero point (no population) and ratios of values are meaningful.

(S) Ranking of a city based on ease of business - (II) Ordinal : Rankings represent ordinal data because they indicate an order or sequence but the differences between ranks are not necessarily equal.

Thus, the correct combination is :

$$ (P)-(I), \; (Q)-(III), \; (R)-(IV), \; (S)-(II) $$
